name: Autorun
summary: The Autorun collector returns the autorun entries for a Windows-managed device.

fields.name: entry_timestamp
fields.description: Last modification time of the autorun entry location. It can be a folder, registry key, or scheduled tasks.

fields.name: entry_location
fields.description: The entry location of the autorun entry. It can be a folder, registry key, or scheduled tasks.

fields.name: entry
fields.description: The name of the entry. It can be the name of the shortcut, registry key name, or task full name.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Indicates if the entry is active.

fields.name: category
fields.description: Specifies the category of the entry. The possible values are: Logon, Explorer, Internet Explorer, Tasks, Codecs, Image Hijack, AppInit Dlls, Known DLLs, Boot Execute, WinLogon, Print Monitors, LSA Providers, Network Providers, Office).

fields.name: Profile
fields.description: Profile indicates the user mode under which we discovered the entry. The possible values are: Logged-on user name, System-wide.

fields.name: Description
fields.description: The description member of the version data for the file associated with the entry.

fields.name: Publisher
fields.description: The publisher member of the version data for the file associated with the entry.

fields.name: image_path
fields.description: The file path for the file associated with the entry.

fields.name: Version
fields.description: The version member of the version data for the file associated with the entry.

fields.name: launch_string
fields.description: The command line with which the file was launched for this entry.

examples.title: Example: Show autorun entries for Task Scheduler
examples.v2_query: AutoRun where AutoRun entry_location equals "Task Scheduler"

examples.title: Which categories of autorun entries exist
examples.v2_query: AutoRun entry, entry_location, image_path, category, enabled

examples.title: Filter by exclusion - by single field with many matches
examples.v2_query:
AutoRun entry, entry_location, image_path, category, enabled
    WHERE AutoRun category not equals "Explorer"
        AND AutoRun category not equals "Codecs"
        AND AutoRun category not equals "Logon"
        AND AutoRun category not equals "Tasks"
        AND AutoRun category not equals "Print Monitors"
        AND AutoRun category not equals "LSA Providers"
        AND AutoRun category not equals "Known DLLs"
        AND AutoRun category not equals "Network Providers"

examples.title: Filter by exclusion - by many fields
examples.v2_query:
AutoRun entry, entry_location, image_path, category, enabled
    WHERE AutoRun enabled not equals "enabled"
        AND AutoRun image_path contains "C:\Users"
        AND AutoRun image_path ends with ".exe"
            AND AutoRun category not equals "Explorer"
            AND AutoRun category not equals "Codecs"
            AND AutoRun category not equals "Logon"
            AND AutoRun category not equals "Tasks"
            AND AutoRun category not equals "Print Monitors"
            AND AutoRun category not equals "LSA Providers"
            AND AutoRun category not equals "Known DLLs"
            AND AutoRun category not equals "Network Providers"

examples.title: Which entries exist for the explorer autorun
examples.v2_query:
AutoRun entry, entry_location, image_path, category
    WHERE AutoRun category equals "Explorer"

examples.title: Which entries exist for the logon autorun
examples.v2_query:
AutoRun entry, entry_location, image_path, category
    WHERE AutoRun category equals "Logon"

examples.title: Which entries exist for the tasks autorun
examples.v2_query:
AutoRun entry, entry_location, image_path, category
    WHERE AutoRun category equals "Tasks"

examples.title: Specify tasks that launch with exe files
examples.v2_query:
AutoRun  category, entry, entry_location, image_path, launch_string, description
    WHERE AutoRun category equals "Tasks"
        AND AutoRun image_path ends with ".exe"

examples.title: Specify tasks that launch with exe files - from user space
examples.v2_query:
AutoRun  category, entry, entry_location, image_path, launch_string, description
    WHERE AutoRun category equals "Tasks"
        AND AutoRun image_path ends with ".exe"
            AND AutoRun image_path contains "C:\Users\"

examples.title: Specify tasks that launch with exe files - by description
examples.v2_query:
AutoRun  category, entry, entry_location, image_path, launch_string, description
    WHERE AutoRun category equals "Tasks"
        AND AutoRun image_path ends with ".exe"
            AND AutoRun description equals "FakeGoogle Installer"
